title: Native constraints and error semantics

publication.changeSummary: Technology-specific HTML: Semantic and Accessible Web Documents draft for Native constraints and error semantics; independent standards, browser, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: HTML Living Standard; living standard accessed 2026-07; provisions applicable to Native constraints and error semantics
sources.claim: Native constraints such as required, type, min, max, and pattern provide browser validation semantics and should be paired with clear instructions and errors.

sources.locator: Web Content Accessibility Guidelines 2.2; W3C Recommendation 2023; provisions applicable to Native constraints and error semantics
sources.claim: Native constraints such as required, type, min, max, and pattern provide browser validation semantics and should be paired with clear instructions and errors.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Native constraints such as required, type, min, max, and pattern provide browser validation semantics and should be paired with clear instructions and errors.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: An email input is labeled, required, and reports a text error linked to the control while the server repeats validation. Your goal is to implement and test native constraints and describe how errors will be identified and corrected. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Paragraph and inline meaning; Relative and absolute URLs; Groups, choices and autocomplete. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: Native constraints such as required, type, min, max, and pattern provide browser validation semantics and should be paired with clear instructions and errors. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: Client validation is bypassable, pattern syntax and locale need care, and invalid styling alone cannot communicate an error. This draft therefore rejects: The required attribute guarantees valid and trustworthy data reaches the server.

includes: Use required, type, range, length and pattern constraints while planning understandable server validation.
Implement and test native constraints and describe how errors will be identified and corrected.
excludes: Client validation is bypassable, pattern syntax and locale need care, and invalid styling alone cannot communicate an error.

outcomes: Implement and test native constraints and describe how errors will be identified and corrected.

examples.title: Native constraints and error semantics: concrete case
examples.situation: An email input is labeled, required, and reports a text error linked to the control while the server repeats validation.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the exact artifact and starting state: An email input is labeled, required, and reports a text error linked to the control while the server repeats validation.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: Use native constraints for user feedback, then perform authoritative validation on the server and communicate errors in text.
examples.result: The result follows because Use native constraints for user feedback, then perform authoritative validation on the server and communicate errors in text.

Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Reject the misconception: The required attribute guarantees valid and trustworthy data reaches the server.

analogy.example: A labeled document outline can model Native constraints and error semantics, with structure carrying meaning before decoration.
usefulFor: Building a first mental model for Native constraints and error semantics before using exact platform terms
limitations: Documents cannot model parser recovery, browser APIs, or interactive semantics exactly.
